categories.name: صِفَاتُ الرَّحْمَةِ وَالْمَغْفِرَةِ
categories.nameEn: Divine Attributes of Mercy and Compassion

items.verse: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ كُتِبَ عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلْقِصَاصُ فِى ٱلْقَتْلَى ۖ ٱلْحُرُّ بِٱلْحُرِّ وَٱلْعَبْدُ بِٱلْعَبْدِ وَٱلْأُنثَىٰ بِٱلْأُنثَىٰ ۚ فَمَنْ عُفِىَ لَهُۥ مِنْ أَخِيهِ شَىْءٌۭ فَٱتِّبَاعٌۢ بِٱلْمَعْرُوفِ وَأَدَآءٌ إِلَيْهِ بِإِحْسَـٰنٍۢ ۗ ذَٰلِكَ تَخْفِيفٌۭ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ وَرَحْمَةٌۭ ۗ فَمَنِ ٱعْتَدَىٰ بَعْدَ ذَٰلِكَ فَلَهُۥ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌۭ
items.verseEn: O you who have believed, prescribed for you is legal retribution for those murdered - the free for the free, the slave for the slave, and the female for the female. But whoever overlooks from his brother [i.e., the killer] anything, then there should be a suitable follow-up and payment to him [i.e., the deceased's heir or legal representative] with good conduct. This is an alleviation from your Lord and a mercy. But whoever transgresses after that will have a painful punishment.

items.verse: فَإِنِ ٱنتَهَوْا۟ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورٌۭ رَّحِيمٌۭ
items.verseEn: And if they cease, then indeed, Allāh is Forgiving and Merciful.

items.verse: فَمَنْ خَافَ مِن مُّوصٍۢ جَنَفًا أَوْ إِثْمًۭا فَأَصْلَحَ بَيْنَهُمْ فَلَآ إِثْمَ عَلَيْهِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورٌۭ رَّحِيمٌۭ
items.verseEn: But if one fears from the bequeather [some] error or sin and corrects that which is between them [i.e., the concerned parties], there is no sin upon him. Indeed, Allāh is Forgiving and Merciful.

items.verse: وَٱسْتَغْفِرُوا۟ رَبَّكُمْ ثُمَّ تُوبُوٓا۟ إِلَيْهِ ۚ إِنَّ رَبِّى رَحِيمٌۭ وَدُودٌۭ
items.verseEn: And ask forgiveness of your Lord and then repent to Him. Indeed, my Lord is Merciful and Affectionate."
And caused to grow within it grain
That We may bring forth thereby grain and vegetation.

items.verse: مُّحَمَّدٌۭ رَّسُولُ ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَٱلَّذِينَ مَعَهُۥٓ أَشِدَّآءُ عَلَى ٱلْكُفَّارِ رُحَمَآءُ بَيْنَهُمْ ۖ تَرَىٰهُمْ رُكَّعًۭا سُجَّدًۭا يَبْتَغُونَ فَضْلًۭا مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ وَرِضْوَٰنًۭا ۖ سِيمَاهُمْ فِى وُجُوهِهِم مِّنْ أَثَرِ ٱلسُّجُودِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ مَثَلُهُمْ فِى ٱلتَّوْرَىٰةِ ۚ وَمَثَلُهُمْ فِى ٱلْإِنجِيلِ كَزَرْعٍ أَخْرَجَ شَطْـَٔهُۥ فَـَٔازَرَهُۥ فَٱسْتَغْلَظَ فَٱسْتَوَىٰ عَلَىٰ سُوقِهِۦ يُعْجِبُ ٱلزُّرَّاعَ لِيَغِيظَ بِهِمُ ٱلْكُفَّارَ ۗ وَعَدَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ مِنْهُم مَّغْفِرَةًۭ وَأَجْرًا عَظِيمًۢا
items.verseEn: Muḥammad is the Messenger of Allāh; and those with him are forceful against the disbelievers, merciful among themselves. You see them bowing and prostrating [in prayer], seeking bounty from Allāh and [His] pleasure. Their sign is in their faces from the effect of prostration [i.e., prayer]. That is their description in the Torah. And their description in the Gospel is as a plant which produces its offshoots and strengthens them so they grow firm and stand upon their stalks, delighting the sowers - so that He [i.e., Allāh] may enrage by them the disbelievers. Allāh has promised those who believe and do righteous deeds among them forgiveness and a great reward.

items.verse: وَكَانَ لَهُۥ ثَمَرٌۭ فَقَالَ لِصَـٰحِبِهِۦ وَهُوَ يُحَاوِرُهُۥٓ أَنَا۠ أَكْثَرُ مِنكَ مَالًۭا وَأَعَزُّ نَفَرًۭا
items.verseEn: And he had fruit, so he said to his companion while he was conversing with him, "I am greater than you in wealth and mightier in [numbers of] men."

items.verse: قُل لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ سَتُغْلَبُونَ وَتُحْشَرُونَ إِلَىٰ جَهَنَّمَ ۚ وَبِئْسَ ٱلْمِهَادُ
items.verseEn: Say to those who disbelieve, "You will be overcome and gathered together to Hell, and wretched is the resting place."
I will drive him into Saqar.
No! He will surely be thrown into the Crusher.

items.verse: وَٱلَّذِينَ سَعَوْا۟ فِىٓ ءَايَـٰتِنَا مُعَـٰجِزِينَ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْجَحِيمِ
items.verseEn: But the ones who strove against Our verses, [seeking] to cause failure - those are the companions of Hellfire.

items.verse: إِنَّمَا يَفْتَرِى ٱلْكَذِبَ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْكَـٰذِبُونَ
items.verseEn: They only invent falsehood who do not believe in the verses of Allāh, and it is those who are the liars.

items.verse: وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ إِنْ هَـٰذَآ إِلَّآ إِفْكٌ ٱفْتَرَىٰهُ وَأَعَانَهُۥ عَلَيْهِ قَوْمٌ ءَاخَرُونَ ۖ فَقَدْ جَآءُو ظُلْمًۭا وَزُورًۭا
items.verseEn: And those who disbelieve say, "This [Qur’ān] is not except a falsehood he invented, and another people assisted him in it." But they have committed an injustice and a lie.

items.verse: وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لِلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَوْ كَانَ خَيْرًۭا مَّا سَبَقُونَآ إِلَيْهِ ۚ وَإِذْ لَمْ يَهْتَدُوا۟ بِهِۦ فَسَيَقُولُونَ هَـٰذَآ إِفْكٌۭ قَدِيمٌۭ
items.verseEn: And those who disbelieve say of those who believe, "If it had [truly] been good, they would not have preceded us to it." And when they are not guided by it, they will say, "This is an ancient falsehood."

items.verse: ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ ٱتَّبَعُوا۟ ٱلْبَـٰطِلَ وَأَنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱتَّبَعُوا۟ ٱلْحَقَّ مِن رَّبِّهِمْ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يَضْرِبُ ٱللَّهُ لِلنَّاسِ أَمْثَـٰلَهُمْ
items.verseEn: That is because those who disbelieve follow falsehood, and those who believe follow the truth from their Lord. Thus does Allāh present to the people their comparisons.

harf.hint: A divine attribute paired with strengthened kingship and decisive speech in describing Prophet Solomon
harf.arabicVerse: وَشَدَدْنَا مُلْكَهُۥ وَءَاتَيْنَـٰهُ ٱلْحِكْمَةَ وَفَصْلَ ٱلْخِطَابِ
harf.verse: 38:20 - And We strengthened his kingdom and gave him wisdom and discernment in speech.

deduction.title: The One Before Whose Signs a Kingdom Bowed
deduction.intro: A remarkable journey awaits those who dare to solve this mystery. Deep in the pages of Revelation, there existed a sovereign whose encounter with a prophet would reshape an entire nation. This figure ruled from a distant throne, guided by ancient traditions, until a extraordinary moment of divine truth transformed both ruler and realm forever. Will you uncover the identity of this remarkable soul whose story bridges the extraordinary and the profound?
clues: I governed a prosperous nation across distant waters, renowned for our gardens and towering palace.
My authority was supreme, yet a great kingdom came to challenge our very existence and identity.
A small bird carried an extraordinary message, and the wonders that followed shattered all my assumptions about power.
When I beheld a throne transformed before my eyes, I knew no mortal could possess such capabilities.
I openly declared my submission to the One Lord of the heavens and earth, abandoning all I once held dear.
We, as a nation, turned away from ancient sun-worship and embraced the truth that changed everything.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: Bilqis – The Queen of Sheba
The Queen of Madyan
A Wife of Prophet Yusuf
The Queen of Egypt
A Princess of Babylon

trial.label: The Great Trial
options: A devastating flood that destroyed the kingdom
Receiving a mysterious message via an unusual bird
A battle against the armies of Prophet Sulayman
Thirty years of severe drought and hardship
An earthquake that swallowed the royal palace
trial.answer: Receiving a mysterious message via an unusual bird

location.label: Key Location
options: The River Nile in ancient Egypt
The Sacred Sanctuary of Makkah
The Courtyard of Prophet Dawud
Yemen and its legendary gardens
The plains of Jerusalem

outcome.label: The Outcome
options: The kingdom was destroyed as punishment for disbelief
I married Prophet Sulayman and lived in Jerusalem
We submitted to Allah and embraced the true faith
I returned to my throne and continued ruling as before
The nation was captured and absorbed into another empire
outcome.answer: We submitted to Allah and embraced the true faith

deduction.arabic: فَقَالَ إِنِّىٓ أَحْبَبْتُ حُبَّ ٱلْخَيْرِ عَن ذِكْرِ رَبِّى حَتَّىٰ تَوَارَتْ بِٱلْحِجَابِ
deduction.verse: And he said, "Indeed, I gave preference to the love of good [things] over the remembrance of my Lord until it [i.e., the sun] disappeared into the curtain [of darkness]." (38:32)

translations: will not be tested will not be tested
scramble.hint: A verse about reliance on Allah (tawakkul) and His promise to support those who turn to Him in faith
scramble.arabic: أَحَسِبَ ٱلنَّاسُ أَن يُتْرَكُوٓا۟ أَن يَقُولُوٓا۟ ءَامَنَّا وَهُمْ لَا يُفْتَنُونَ
scramble.verseEn: Do the people think that they will be left to say, "We believe" and they will not be tried?
